purpose: Claude Code skill definition for `master-protocol`. It describes trigger conditions, workflow, constraints, and referenced protocol files for that skill. Key content: --- name: master-protocol description: > Synapse master orchestrator protocol. Loaded automatically when operating as a swarm master agent. Contains role constraints, dashboard write schemas, dispatch rules, module index, and common pitfalls. user-invocable: false --- The file is 210 lines long and should be treated as a knowledge/control-plane source rather than application code.
gotchas: Legacy task-record reference: this file mentions tasks/{date}/parallel_* artifacts; current p_track planning context is canonical in dashboards/{dashboardId}/plan.json, with lifecycle state in progress files.
patterns: Skill-definition pattern: trigger guidance first, then workflow steps and referenced protocol files; these files are loaded on demand by Claude Code.
Uses explicit NON-NEGOTIABLE language to encode hard behavioral constraints for agents.
Uses markdown tables for compact command, file, or option matrices.
conventions: Markdown headings use a single H1 followed by H2/H3 sections for navigable documentation.
Agent-control documents use `{tracker_root}`, `{project_root}`, and `{dashboardId}` placeholders instead of machine-specific paths.
ASCII double hyphen is used in prose where many docs avoid Unicode punctuation for terminal readability.
